questions.q: Explain the concept of Planning as a principle of management. What are the steps involved in the planning process?
questions.answer: Planning is the first and most important principle of management. It involves determining the goals and objectives of an organization. The steps involved in the planning process are: (i) Establishing the mission and vision of the organization, (ii) Identifying the present situation, (iii) Forecasting the future, (iv) Determining the goals and objectives, (v) Formulating a plan, and (vi) Implementing and controlling the plan.
marking_scheme: Describing the concept of planning (1 mark)
Mentioning the steps involved in the planning process (2 marks)
Elaborating on any one step (2 marks)
questions.topic: Planning as a Principle of Management

questions.q: What is the role of Organizing in an organization? Explain the different types of departments that can be formed in an organization.
questions.answer: Organizing is the second principle of management. It involves dividing and grouping of activities into departments and assigning responsibilities to each department. The different types of departments that can be formed in an organization are: (i) Functional departments, (ii) Divisional departments, (iii) Product departments, and (iv) Geographic departments.
marking_scheme: Describing the role of organizing (1 mark)
Mentioning the different types of departments (2 marks)
Elaborating on any one type of department (2 marks)
questions.topic: Organizing as a Principle of Management

questions.q: Explain the concept of Motivation as a principle of management. What are the different types of motivators?
questions.answer: Motivation is the sixth principle of management. It involves stimulating the workers to work towards the goals of the organization. The different types of motivators are: (i) Financial motivators, (ii) Non-financial motivators, (iii) Intrinsic motivators, and (iv) Extrinsic motivators.
marking_scheme: Describing the concept of motivation (1 mark)
Mentioning the different types of motivators (2 marks)
Elaborating on any one type of motivator (2 marks)
questions.topic: Motivation as a Principle of Management

questions.q: A company has a profit of ₹ 1,00,000 and an expense of ₹ 80,000. What is the contribution margin?
questions.answer:
Calculation: Contribution Margin = Sales - Variable Expenses
Contribution Margin = ₹ 1,00,000 - ₹ 80,000 = ₹ 20,000

questions.q: A company sells 1,000 units of a product at ₹ 100 each, with a total fixed cost of ₹ 50,000. What is the break-even point in rupees?
questions.answer:
Calculation: Break-even point = Total Fixed Cost / Contribution Margin per unit
First, calculate the Contribution Margin per unit: CM per unit = Selling price per unit - Variable cost per unit
Assuming variable cost per unit is ₹ 50, since selling price is ₹ 100, and contribution margin is ₹ 50.
CM per unit = ₹ 100 - ₹ 50 = ₹ 50
Now, calculate the Break-even point:
Break-even point = ₹ 50,000 / ₹ 50 = 1,000 units
Since the question asks for break-even point in rupees, multiply the units by the selling price:
Break-even point in rupees = 1,000 units × ₹ 100 = ₹ 1,00,000
marking_scheme: Calculating the contribution margin per unit (2 marks)

questions.q: A company has an average product price of ₹ 500 and a total fixed cost of ₹ 75,000. What is the break-even point in units?
questions.answer:
Calculation: Break-even point = Total Fixed Cost / Contribution Margin per unit
Contribution Margin per unit = Selling price per unit - Variable cost per unit
Assuming variable cost per unit is ₹ 500 - x, where x is the contribution margin per unit.
Since it is given that the contribution margin is ₹ 500 - x and the selling price is ₹ 500, Contribution Margin per unit = ₹ 500 - x = ₹ 500 - ₹ 500 = ₹ 0
This shows that variable cost per unit is equal to the selling price per unit, which is not possible in a business situation. Thus, it is assumed that variable cost per unit is less than the selling price per unit.
Let's assume that variable cost per unit is ₹ 450, then Contribution Margin per unit = ₹ 500 - ₹ 450 = ₹ 50
Now, calculate the Break-even point:
Break-even point = ₹ 75,000 / ₹ 50 = 1,500 units
marking_scheme: Calculating the contribution margin per unit (2 marks)
